coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Sunday in peak phase = second long effort or recovery. Given you're on day 7 of a hard week, run 75–90 minutes at Z2 (140–155 bpm) on rolling terrain with 600–800m of vert. This is active recovery with purpose: your aerobic base is locked in, and Sunday's job is to flush lactate from yesterday's long run while maintaining specificity for alpine terrain. Do NOT chase pace; nail the vert and hold conversational effort. If legs feel heavy, dial it back to 60 minutes easy—listen to your body, not the plan.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You've run hard Saturday and will again Tuesday. Prioritize glycogen repletion today: 8–10g carbs per kg body weight over the next 24 hours (roughly 540–675g if you're 67–70kg). Front-load carbs in the 2–3 hours post-run: rice, pasta, fruit, or sports drink. For Sunday's 90-minute effort itself, sip 250ml of 6% carb drink every 20 minutes if running in heat; plain water is fine in cool alpine conditions. Evening: hit 30–40g protein to support muscle repair from the week's cumulative load.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: You're in peak phase with 103 days to race—taper is 3 weeks away. Prioritize 7.5–8.5 hours of sleep tonight; peak training + travel stress taxes CNS recovery hard. If travel lag is present, add a 20–30 minute nap before Sunday's run. Consider 10–15 minutes of easy foam rolling on quads, calves, and IT band post-run (not aggressive). Skip ice baths this week; they blunt adaptation during peak phase. Active mobility (hip opener, calf stretch) for 5 minutes beats nothing.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 14 weeks out with 3 solid peak weeks logged. Julian Alps will demand patience on descents (technical, loose scree), early pacing discipline (hold back the first 20K), and willingness to suffer between aid stations. Use today's Sunday run to practice staying composed when tired—that's the mental skill the race will test. Visualize one specific technical descent or exposed ridge on the route; rehearse your line and breathing cue. No heroics; controlled effort wins alpine ultras.
